title: Was it all in Shinjis head
display_title: Was it all in Shinjis head
wikitext:
[]: Was it all in Shinji's head             Quote Post by Zahrim » Sun Apr 01, 2007 9:53 pm Ok heres the deal im a massive eva fan i watched it since it first aired and still watch it atleast 3 times a year now a guy has come along who thinks he knows more than me (hes a know it all he actualy tried to convince me that in warhammer 40k the race of immortals the necrons have never been alive they were just robots which has been proven wrong and he thinks hes right still) ok well he has tried to make me beleive lke him but ive come here to prove him wrong by a general concensus was the entire series of evangelion imagined by Shinji and the final episode justifies this or is it as i beleive the final episode was a way of him regaining his sanity and understanding of the world

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Apr 01, 2007 9:56 pm if you're talking about EoTV (the tv episodes), then... i guess most of it was in Shinji's head. But that's way to simple of an answer to just take at face value. I'll give you some advice, You and your friend spend about a week reading through the discussion section of these boards, at all sorts of things, and play around with the search function. If you still have questions after that week, then ask them. This isn't a rule or anything, just some advice.

[]:             Quote Post by ThroneofDravaris » Sun Apr 01, 2007 10:17 pm Err...if you want, sure.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sun Apr 01, 2007 10:36 pm I wouldn't be surprised if Webuild turns out with it all being a figment of some character's imagination. However, for Shin Seiki, most of it was reality. The obvious fantasy things would be the various dream sequences. As well as episodes 25 and 26 where Shinji is coming to terms with all that's happened in the past along with some of the present happenings in 25' and 26'(EoE).

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Apr 01, 2007 10:41 pm Well, counter arguments against the "Oh, everything is all in this person's head, none of it is real" usually require outside sources, like notes or interviews with the creators. Since the assumption is everything in the series is just inside Shinji's head makes it so anything I use from within the series to counter this assumption leads to a, "well, that was in his head"-esque reply. If this person is coming from this kinda of approach, then there's no point in arguing. However, I am mildly curious how the last 2 episodes of the TV series "proves" this, because you know, there was this instrumentality thing lingering behind the shadows all throughout the show. You lose physical form, merge into a single being with every other human, that type of thing.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Apr 01, 2007 10:42 pm slothen wrote: if you're talking about EoTV (the tv episodes), then... i guess most of it was in Shinji's head. But that's way to simple of an answer to just take at face value.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Apr 01, 2007 10:47 pm slothen wrote: When I say this, I mean it was told from Shinji's perspective.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Sun Apr 01, 2007 10:47 pm Zahrim wrote: was the entire series of evangelion imagined by Shinji and the final episode justifies this or is it as i beleive the final episode was a way of him regaining his sanity and understanding of the world

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Apr 01, 2007 10:50 pm Reichu wrote: slothen wrote: When I say this, I mean it was told from Shinji's perspective.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Sun Apr 01, 2007 10:55 pm THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: I wouldn't be surprised if Webuild turns out with it all being a figment of some character's imagination.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sun Apr 01, 2007 11:09 pm BrikHaus81 wrote: I wrote: I wouldn't be surprised if Webuild turns out with it all being a figment of some character's imagination.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Apr 02, 2007 3:23 am Of course it was all in Shinji's head -- just that a lot of other people were also using it at the same time. Some of the few clear bits of WTF is going on are the explanations in 25 where Gendo, then Misato and Ritsuko, describe the Instrumentality project.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Apr 02, 2007 11:33 am If you mean it was "all in Shinji's head" in the sense that "all of humanity was one mentally so we can see each other's thoughts and feelings" (well, once we get to the middle of 25 when Shinji's tanged, that is) , then yes you're right. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Mon Apr 02, 2007 1:47 pm Are you saying 'the whole series was in Shinji's head' in that case, no. Or are you saying all of EoTV was in Shinji's head. In that case, no. SEE ABOVE, SEE EOE
